name: Costco Wholesale Corporation v. Omega, S.A.
question: Under the first-sale doctrine of copyright law, someone who purchases a copyrighted work (like a book) can later sell the work to someone else without the permission of the copyright holder (the book's author). Does the "first-sale doctrine" apply to imported works manufactured abroad?
description: A case in which the Court held that the first-sale doctrine did not apply to imported goods manufactured abroad. This case set no new Supreme Court precedent.
